mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129526.0 thousand as of August 2015
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth does not correlate with subsequent 20-day SPX returns at Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS months produce negative SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), signaling continued monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and sustains equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering a negative reassessment of corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355546.0 million and HOUST at 591.0 thousand as of August 2015
failure_condition: Simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month trend, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and profit margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds its 3-month moving average by > 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration events do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) creates stagflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to signal rate increases, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.587 and UNRATE at 9.7% as of August 2015
regime_hint: stagflationary regime or policy tightening cycle
failure_condition: Stagflationary trigger conditions do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 65% of instances, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum that reduces forward earnings estimates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13161.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of April 2015
failure_condition: Sub-2% GDPC1 growth does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, signaling labor market deterioration and triggering a risk-off equity market response that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.3 percentage points over the preceding 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or shock-driven contraction
failure_condition: UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 62% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above their 6-month trend while retail sales (RSAFS) remain stable or rising, signaling robust consumer demand and housing-led growth that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth exceeds its 6-month moving average AND RSAFS does not decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 591.0 thousand and RSAFS at 355546.0 million as of August 2015
regime_hint: housing-led expansion or post-crisis recovery acceleration
failure_condition: Joint HOUST acceleration and RSAFS stability do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 65% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.
